title: hieracium rectum subsp. myriophyllum
intro: Hieracium rectum subsp. myriophyllum, also known as hawkweed, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its yellow or orange flowers and fern-like leaves. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Consuming hawkweed has been linked to several health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune system function. Hawkweed can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine and folk remedies.
